draft:
Dear Officer:

I am writing this letter to provide my strongest recommendation for Ms. Akshara Malhotra as an individual of extraordinary ability in the field of biotechnology. I have had the privilege of working closely with Ms. Malhotra through the IGNITE Translational Research Program at Crestford University School of Medicine, where she has served as an advisor since 2016. Her contributions to the program and her impressive career in the biotechnology industry make her an outstanding candidate for the visa classification she seeks.

By way of background, I am a Professor of Chemical & Systems Biology at Crestford University School of Medicine, where I have been on the faculty since 2008. I earned my Bachelor of Science in Biology and Doctor of Medicine from Claremont University, and my Master of Business Administration from Crestford University Graduate School of Business. In addition to my academic roles, I have extensive experience in the biotechnology industry, having served as the Chief Medical Officer for Syntex, Inc. and as the Chief Scientific Officer and Co-Founder of Quantum Biosciences, a division of Quantum International. I also have significant experience in venture capital, having worked as a Venture Partner at Crest Ventures and as a Principal at Olympus Ventures.

At Crestford, I have been deeply involved in translational research and entrepreneurship. I have served as the Co-Director of the IGNITE Translational Research Program since 2008, where I have worked closely with faculty, students, and industry partners to advance promising discoveries from the lab to the clinic. I have also been a member of the Crestford Bio-X Leadership Council and the Crestford Cardiovascular Institute, and I have taught courses on drug development and entrepreneurship at the Crestford Graduate School of Business.

Through my work at IGNITE, I have had the opportunity to collaborate with Ms. Malhotra and witness her extraordinary abilities firsthand. IGNITE is a unique program that provides funding, mentorship, and educational resources to help Crestford faculty and students translate their research into clinical and commercial applications. As an advisor, Ms. Malhotra has played a critical role in the success of the program by providing her expert guidance and support to numerous projects.

Ms. Malhotra's extensive experience in the biotechnology industry has been invaluable to IGNITE. She currently serves as the Director of Portfolio Planning and Program Management at Pinnacle Therapeutics, a leading biotechnology company focused on developing therapies for neurodegenerative diseases. In this role, she leads the strategic planning and execution of Pinnacle's portfolio of drug development programs, working closely with cross-functional teams to advance these programs from discovery through clinical development. Her deep understanding of the drug development process and her ability to integrate scientific and business perspectives have been instrumental in driving the success of Pinnacle's pipeline.

Prior to joining Pinnacle, Ms. Malhotra held various roles of increasing responsibility at Lumigen, Inc., where she ultimately served as the Associate Director of Assay Sciences and Innovation and Head of Research Operations. During her tenure at Lumigen, she led the development of a cutting-edge antibody discovery technology that secured significant funding from the Andrew and Martha Gates Foundation and spearheaded a bacterial genetics project that aimed to create a novel, cost-effective starting point for one of the company's lead candidate antibiotics. Her ability to lead cross-functional teams and drive innovative projects to fruition was evident throughout her time at Lumigen.

Ms. Malhotra's impressive track record in the biotechnology industry is further supported by her strong academic background. She holds a Master of Business Administration from the University of California, Avery, Haas School of Business, where she was awarded the Dean's Scholarship and Forté Fellowship for her exemplary leadership in business and academic excellence. She also holds a Master of Biotechnology from The Pennsylvania State Institute, where she achieved a near-perfect GPA, and a Bachelor of Technology in Biotechnology from Marintree University in Chennai, India, where she graduated with academic distinction and was awarded a full tuition scholarship for her outstanding academic performance.

At IGNITE, Ms. Malhotra has leveraged her extensive industry experience and academic background to provide invaluable guidance and support to the program's participants. As an advisor, she has reviewed current projects and offered expert feedback on various aspects of development, such as clinical trial design, commercialization strategies, and scientific progress. Her ability to integrate scientific and business perspectives has been particularly valuable in helping IGNITE scholars navigate the complex landscape of drug development and bring their innovations closer to the clinic.

In addition to providing feedback on existing projects, Ms. Malhotra has also been instrumental in selecting new projects for the program. Her keen eye for identifying promising technologies and her deep understanding of the market landscape have ensured that IGNITE continues to support high-potential projects that address significant unmet medical needs. Her contributions to the project selection process have helped maintain the program's high standards and its reputation as a leading translational research initiative.

Beyond her formal responsibilities as an advisor, Ms. Malhotra has been a generous mentor to many IGNITE scholars, providing one-on-one guidance and support to help them advance their projects. Her approachable demeanor and willingness to share her expertise have made her a trusted resource for participants, who greatly appreciate her insights and encouragement. Her mentorship has been particularly valuable for early-career researchers, who benefit from her guidance on navigating the challenges of translational research and building successful careers in the biotechnology industry.

Ms. Malhotra's contributions to IGNITE extend beyond her individual interactions with participants. She has also played a key role in fostering a collaborative and supportive environment within the program. Her ability to bring together diverse stakeholders, including faculty, students, and industry experts, has helped create a vibrant community where ideas can be freely exchanged and collaborations can flourish. Her efforts to build and strengthen this community have been instrumental in the program's success.

In addition to her work with IGNITE, Ms. Malhotra has been involved in several other initiatives that demonstrate her commitment to advancing the field of biotechnology and supporting the next generation of leaders. She serves as a Biotechnology Advisory Board Member at The Pennsylvania State Institute, where she provides guidance on curriculum development and industry partnerships. She is also a Start-up Advisor for VentureSpring, where she mentors women-led life sciences startups, helping them refine their business strategies and connect with potential investors and partners.

Ms. Malhotra's contributions to the field of biotechnology have been recognized through several awards and honors. At UC Avery, she was awarded the Dean's Scholarship and Forté Fellowship for her exemplary leadership in business and academic excellence. These prestigious awards are given to a select group of students who demonstrate outstanding potential for leadership in the business world. Her receipt of these awards is a testament to her exceptional abilities and her commitment to advancing the field of biotechnology.

In addition to these formal recognitions, Ms. Malhotra's work has been featured in several high-profile media outlets. Her role in the launch of GeneHack, a CRISPR biotech startup, was highlighted in publications such as BioSphere, Businesswave, and Medical Innovations Insider. GeneHack, which she co-founded with Dr. Clarke Williams from the Innovators Genomics Institute, focuses on developing a class of peptides capable of conveying CRISPR enzymes into clinically-relevant and previously untransfectable cell types. The company's innovative approach to overcoming delivery challenges in genome editing has garnered significant attention and praise from the scientific community and the media.

Furthermore, GeneHack was selected for transcription as part of the Avery Haas Case Series, "Clearing Hurdles to Academic Startup Formation: How GeneHack Became a Company," published in collaboration with UC Avery Chancellor Jared Lyons. This case study highlights GeneHack's pioneering approach to therapeutic innovation and its significant contribution to shaping university-driven entrepreneurship models. The inclusion of GeneHack in this prestigious series underscores the impact of Ms. Malhotra's work and her ability to drive innovation in the biotechnology industry.

In conclusion, Ms. Malhotra is a truly extraordinary individual whose contributions to the field of biotechnology have had a significant impact on both the academic and industry landscapes. Her extensive experience in drug development, her impressive academic background, and her unwavering commitment to advancing translational research make her an invaluable asset to any organization. Her work with IGNITE, her leadership at Pinnacle Therapeutics, and her role in launching GeneHack demonstrate her ability to drive innovation and bring transformative therapies to patients in need.

Ms. Malhotra's unique combination of scientific expertise, business acumen, and dedication to mentorship and collaboration sets her apart as a leader in the biotechnology field. Her ability to bridge the gap between academia and industry, her talent for identifying and advancing promising technologies, and her commitment to supporting the next generation of scientists and entrepreneurs make her an outstanding candidate for the visa classification she seeks.

I have no doubt that Ms. Malhotra will continue to make significant contributions to the biotechnology industry and to the advancement of human health. Her presence in the United States will undoubtedly benefit the scientific community and the broader society. I wholeheartedly recommend Ms. Malhotra for the visa classification she seeks and urge you to give her application your full consideration.

Please feel free to contact me if you require any further information or clarification. I would be happy to provide additional details or discuss Ms. Malhotra's qualifications further.

Sincerely,

Liam Harrison, MD, MBA

Professor of Chemical & Systems Biology

Crestford University School of Medicine

Co-Director, IGNITE Translational Research Program

Crestford, California, USA

[email contact here]
final:
Dear Officer:

	This letter is intended to serve as my wholehearted recommendation for Ms. Akshara Malhotra's visa petition. Ms. Malhotra is a remarkable individual with expertise in the highly critical and complex fields of biopharmaceuticals, neurodegenerative disorders and infectious diseases. Further, she serves as a crucial advisor of innovative biotech startups, significantly contributing to innovation in the field. I have had the privilege of working closely with Ms. Malhotra through the IGNITE Translational Research Program at Crestford University School of Medicine, where she has served as an advisor since 2016. Her contributions to the program and her impressive career in the biotechnology industry truly set her apart from others in the field.

 By way of background, I am a Professor of Chemical & Systems Biology at Crestford University School of Medicine, where I have been on the faculty since 2008. At Crestford, I have been deeply involved in translational research and entrepreneurship. I have also served as the Co-Director of the IGNITE Translational Research Program since 2008, where I have worked closely with faculty, students, and industry partners to advance promising discoveries from the lab to the clinic. Further, I serve as an Advisor at SparkPoint NS, an independent translational research organization founded in 2023 that is dedicated to efficiently advancing promising neuroscience discoveries.

	Through my work at IGNITE, I have had the opportunity to collaborate with Ms. Malhotra and witness her extraordinary abilities firsthand. Securing membership as an Advisor at IGNITE at Crestford is a testament to her remarkable expertise and dedication. IGNITE is a unique program that provides funding, mentorship, and educational resources to help Crestford faculty and students translate their research into clinical and commercial applications. As an advisor, Ms. Malhotra has played a critical role in the success of the program by providing her expert guidance and support to numerous projects. Her ability to offer insightful feedback on project development, including clinical trial design and commercialization strategies, has significantly advanced the scientific and market potential of various initiatives.

	Ms. Malhotra's involvement goes beyond mere advisory; she is instrumental in identifying and selecting new projects that align with both scientific innovation and market needs, ensuring that the initiatives undertaken by IGNITE are of the highest potential. Her commitment to mentoring projects on a one-on-one basis exemplifies her dedication to nurturing the next generation of breakthroughs in therapeutics and diagnostics. Moreover, Ms. Malhotra's extensive network has been invaluable in facilitating connections with potential partners, collaborators, or investors, often laying the groundwork for the formation of new companies around promising assets. Her contributions have not only empowered individual projects but have elevated the overall impact and reach of the IGNITE program, showcasing her as a vital asset to its continued success.

	Ms. Malhotra's extensive experience in the biotechnology industry has been invaluable to IGNITE. She currently serves as the Director of Portfolio and Program Strategy at Pinnacle Therapeutics where she manages the strategic planning and execution of a neuroscience portfolio focused on therapies for conditions like Alzheimer’s disease and other neurodegenerative disorders. Her efforts advance investigational products from discovery through clinical development, addressing public health challenges given the vast number of people affected and the significant economic burden. Under her leadership, Pinnacle, a leading company, is at the forefront of developing transformative therapies that cross the blood-brain barrier, crucial for meeting patient and societal needs.

	In her role, Ms. Malhotra guides therapies from research through regulatory pathways, ensuring alignment with corporate objectives. She leads cross-functional teams and utilizes data-driven decision-making to maximize program success. Her work with business development teams enhances Pinnacle’s pipeline by integrating external opportunities. By maintaining a mission-aligned portfolio strategy, she ensures Pinnacle's vision of combating neurodegeneration is realized globally. Her ability to bridge science and strategy not only strengthens Pinnacle's impact but also serves as an inspiration within the IGNITE program and the wider biotech community.

	In addition to her remarkable work at Pinnacle and IGNITE, Ms. Malhotra co-founded a pioneering CRISPR biotech startup alongside Dr. Clarke Williams, called GeneHack. This venture is dedicated to tackling the complex challenges of delivery and advancing precision genome editing, gaining considerable attention from media outlets like BioSphere and Businesswave. Its innovative approach to therapeutic development has been recognized as a key case study in biotech entrepreneurship. Notably, the startup's journey was featured in the Avery Haas Case Series under the title "Clearing Hurdles to Academic Startup Formation: How GeneHack Became a Company," a project developed with insight from UC Avery Chancellor Jared Lyons. This recognition underscores Ms. Malhotra's influence in shaping university-driven entrepreneurial models and has earned the startup a place in the "Opportunity Recognition" MBA curriculum at Avery Haas, serving as an educational resource for future business leaders.

 Ms. Malhotra's impressive track record in the biotechnology industry is further supported by her strong academic background. She holds a Master of Business Administration from the University of California, Avery, Haas School of Business, where she was awarded the Dean's Scholarship and Forté Fellowship for her exemplary leadership in business and academic excellence. She also holds a Master of Biotechnology from The Pennsylvania State Institute, and a Bachelor of Technology in Biotechnology from Marintree University in Chennai, India.

	Ms. Malhotra's remarkable contributions to both industry and academia position her as a leader in the biotechnology field. Her combination of extensive industry experience and strong academic credentials uniquely enables her to bridge the gap between scientific innovation and practical application. She exemplifies a unique blend of technical knowledge and strategic business acumen, which makes her a sought-after expert. Her work consistently demonstrates her ability to drive advancements while nurturing the next generation of biotech entrepreneurs, reinforcing her status as a pillar of innovation. Her impact extends beyond personal achievements, inspiring colleagues and the broader biotech community to strive for excellence.

	In conclusion, Ms. Malhotra is an extraordinary individual whose contributions have greatly impacted both academic and industry landscapes. Her extensive experience in drug development, coupled with her impressive academic background and commitment to advancing translational research, makes her a valuable asset to any organization. Her involvement with IGNITE, leadership at Pinnacle Therapeutics, and role in launching GeneHack demonstrate her ability to drive innovation and deliver transformative therapies to patients.

	I have no doubt that Ms. Malhotra will continue to make significant contributions to the biotechnology industry and the advancement of human health. Her presence in the United States unquestionably benefits the scientific community and society as a whole. Please feel free to contact me if you need further information or clarification.
 
Sincerely,


Liam Harrison
Professor of Chemical & Systems Biology
Crestford University School of Medicine
Co-Director, IGNITE Translational Research Program
[insert contact information]
